text: Technology and disruption risks represent a material concern for Apple, particularly regarding AI execution. According to recent market commentary, Apple has fallen behind in the AI arms race, which has contributed to investor bearishness on the stock. While the broader market rallied from its lows in April and May 2025, Apple's stock remained relatively flat, down 15% for the year at the time of reporting. This suggests competitive pressure in artificial intelligence capabilities could impact future product differentiation and market positioning. The company's R&D intensity of 9.43% in Q3 2025 indicates substantial investment in innovation, though execution risk remains elevated given the rapid pace of AI development across the technology sector.
